description: Tool call with no system prompt — does the model stay in English and use tools rather than switching language?

messages.content: List the files in the current directory.

function.name: bash
function.description: Run a shell command; returns stdout+stderr.

function.name: read
function.description: Read a file; returns contents with line numbers.

expect.notes: Target quirk: Qwen-family models with weak or absent system prompts may produce Chinese-language content or mixed Chinese/English output, because training distribution is Chinese/English and the system prompt is the primary language anchor. Observe: (1) is content null/empty (correct silent tool call) or does it contain non-English text? (2) does the model call a tool at all, or produce a prose response in Chinese? Qwen2.5 had a default system message that anchored language; Qwen3 removed it, making this regression more likely.
